title: Money in Judicial Elections and Court Capture

state_supreme_court_spending.interest_group_share: 54% of all spending in 2023-24 — first time outside groups outspent candidates

most_expensive_races.total: $51M (5x prior state record, 3x prior national record)

judicial_crisis_network_concord_fund.payments_to_leo_firms: $7.7M to CRC Advisors, $500K to BH Group

dark_money_in_judicial_races.arabella_network: $1.5B+ collective revenue in 2024 (Sixteen Thirty Fund, New Venture Fund, etc.)

federalist_society_leonard_leo.trump_judges: 86% of Trump circuit/SCOTUS appointees were Federalist Society members

key_donors.mercer_family: $6M to Federalist Society since 2013; $100M total to right-wing groups
key_donors.charles_koch: $10M donation coordinated by Leo; $20M from anonymous donor for Scalia Law School
key_donors.schwab_charitable: $250M+ to 85 Fund (Leo-connected); $141M in one year = 95% of 85 Fund revenue

marble_freedom_trust.donor: Barre Seid (Chicago manufacturing magnate, CEO of Tripp Lite)
marble_freedom_trust.amount: $1.65B (largest known political advocacy donation in US history)
marble_freedom_trust.mechanism: Transferred 100% ownership of Tripp Lite to 501(c)(4) before Eaton Corporation acquired it for $1.65B

scotus_confirmation_spending.kavanaugh_2018: $10M+ JCN support; another $17M from single donor
scotus_confirmation_spending.barrett_2020: $10M JCN campaign; Concord Fund raised $48.1M that fiscal year
scotus_confirmation_spending.jackson_2022: $1M initial Demand Justice ads; $7.5M projected budget (dwarfed by conservative spending)
scotus_confirmation_spending.concord_fund_downstream_2021: $9M to One Nation (McConnell), $4.8M to RAGA, $2.2M to SBA List, $1.9M to Heritage Action

recusal_conflicts.caperton_v_massey_2009: Blankenship spent $3M electing Justice Benjamin who then voted to overturn $50M verdict against Blankenship's company; SCOTUS ruled Due Process requires recusal in 'extreme facts'
recusal_conflicts.wisconsin_protasiewicz_2023: Dem Party contributed $10M (57% of campaign spending); Republicans demanded recusal from redistricting cases; she declined; court struck down GOP maps

corporate_funded_races.tort_reform_origins: ATRA founded 1986 by tobacco, insurance, chemical, auto, pharma industries
corporate_funded_races.ohio_2024: Chamber-affiliated $2.3M for GOP ($1M from Uihlein); Bloomberg-funded $2M for Dems
corporate_funded_races.energy_sector: $100M+ annual lobbying; Leo groups campaigning to get SCOTUS to block state climate lawsuits
corporate_funded_races.impact: After Chamber flipped Ohio court, state upheld new damages caps in 2007 (had struck them down 1999)

academic_evidence.kang_shepherd_2011: Every additional dollar from business = increased probability of voting for business litigants
academic_evidence.kang_shepherd_2014: Doubling TV ads = 8% increase in judges voting against criminal defendants; post-Citizens United: 7 additional anti-defendant votes per 100 cases
academic_evidence.public_perception: 76% of voters believe contributions influence decisions; 46% of JUDGES acknowledge 'at least a little influence'
academic_evidence.ohio_nyt_2006: Justices voted for contributors 70% of the time
academic_evidence.georgia_2009: Demonstrated campaign contributions directly affect judicial decision-making (causal, not just correlation)

2023_2026_developments.wisconsin_2023: $51M total; Protasiewicz raised $17.44M (6x previous record); 69+ outside groups spent $29M; flipped court liberal 4-3
2023_2026_developments.wisconsin_2025: $100M+ total; Crawford defeated Schimel; Musk spent $18.7M through PACs + $3M to WI GOP + $3M in petition checks; maintained liberal majority
2023_2026_developments.pennsylvania_2023: $27M total; Yass funneled $16M through Students First PAC
2023_2026_developments.pennsylvania_2025: $18.7M on retention elections (record for judicial retention)
2023_2026_developments.abortion_ads: 30% of TV ad spots mentioned abortion (up from 3% in 2020)
2023_2026_developments.trend: Billionaire proxy battles (Musk, Soros, Yass, Uihlein, Pritzker, Bloomberg) have transformed judicial elections
